title: Getting Started with turbo — A Practical Guide for Tools Developers
description: Step-by-step guide to getting started with turbo. Learn setup, configuration, and best practices for Tools.

sourceData.name: turbo
sourceData.description: Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript and TypeScript codebases.

bodyHtml:
turbo is making waves in the Tools ecosystem this week, gaining 16.4MM weekly downloads. accruing over 30.6k stars on GitHub, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 30.6kStars
📈 +16.4M/wkGrowth
🔀 2.4kForks
⚠️ 22Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript/TypeScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://turborepo.dev

Why turbo Is Trending

The Tools landscape is constantly evolving, and turbo has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 16.4M new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript and TypeScript codebases.

If you're working in Tools, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Practical Use Cases

Here are some common scenarios where turbo shines:

  1. New projects: Start your next JavaScript/TypeScript project with this tool as a foundation
  2. Migration: Consider switching if your current solution is slowing you down
  3. Learning: Study the source code to understand modern Tools patterns

Quick Start

Get started with turbo in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://www.npmjs.com/package/turbo # Navigate to project cd turbo # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

turbo is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
